---
title: "Reviewing one paper"
date: "Week 07"
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: true   
    scrollable: true
    smaller: true
    fontsize: 47px
    chalkboard: true
    theme: default
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  eval: false
---

## Recap
| 1 and 2 | "All models are wrong, some are useful" (Box) | Your model doesn't need to be *true* — it needs to be *defensible* |
| 2 | Bayesian vs. frequentist: different treatments of uncertainty | You should be able to say *which framework* you're working in and *why* |
| 3-4 | The Method-Question-Data Triangle | If these three don't align, your results are unreliable |
| 5 | Split-plot, mixed models vs. ANOVA | The same data can be analyzed multiple ways — the *design* dictates the right one |


##
